response:
THE LAW OF MOORE, FORMULATED BY GORDON ECKERT MOORE IN 1965, PREDICTS THAT THE NUMBER OF TRANSISTORS ON A MICROPROCESSOR WILL DOUBLE EVERY 18 TO 24 MONTHS, LEADING TO EXPONENTIAL GROWTH IN COMPUTING POWER. THIS LAW HAS BEEN A DRIVING FORCE BEHIND THE RAPID ADVANCEMENT OF TECHNOLOGY OVER THE PAST DECADES. HOWEVER, AS WE APPROACH THE PHYSICAL LIMITS OF SILICON BASED TECHNOLOGY, IT BECOMES CLEAR THAT THE LAW MAY NOT HOLD INDEFINITELY. SCIENTISTS AND ENGINEERS ARE NOW EXPLORING ALTERNATIVE MATERIALS AND NEW ARCHITECTURES TO CONTINUE THE ADVANCEMENT OF COMPUTING POWER.

***
THE EXACT POINT AT WHICH THE LAW OF MOORE WILL CEASE TO BE VALID IS DIFFICULT TO PREDICT, AS IT DEPENDS ON A VARIETY OF FACTORS INCLUDING MATERIAL SCIENCE, MANUFACTURING TECHNOLOGIES, AND INNOVATIVE DESIGN APPROACHES. WHILE SOME ESTIMATES SUGGEST THAT THE LAW MAY BEGIN TO SLOW DOWN BY THE MID-2020S, OTHERS BELIEVE THAT NEW BREAKTHROUGHS IN QUANTUM COMPUTING, NANOTECHNOLOGY, AND 3D INTEGRATION MAY EXTEND THE LAW'S RELEVANCE FOR DECADES TO COME. IN ANY CASE, THE LAW OF MOORE HAS BEEN A GUIDING PRINCIPLE IN THE EVOLUTION OF COMPUTING, AND ITS IMPACT WILL BE FEELT FOR YEARS TO COME. COMPUTING POWER.
